disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Thick greasy yellow-brown crusting on the scalp vertex, present about 4 weeks, milder at onset
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe what his scalp looks like?
When did the crusting on his head first start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The top of his head has these thick, greasy, yellowish crusts. They started about a month ago but were only small at first.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Post-auricular intertriginous erythema with serous weeping and a small fissure that bled with rubbing
trigger_question_examples: Is there any redness or weeping anywhere else, like in the skin folds?
Tell me about the skin behind his ears.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Behind both his ears the skin is really red and raw and it's been weeping clear fluid. Tonight there was a tiny crack that bled a bit when he rubbed it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Afebrile on home thermometer; no vomiting; well-perfused and breathing comfortably — pertinent negatives for systemic infection
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken his temperature — does he have a fever?
Is he floppy, or has he been vomiting, or breathing fast?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We checked his temperature and it was normal, 36.8, so no fever. He hasn't vomited and his breathing looks fine — he's just so unsettled and itchy.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really checked anything like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reduced feeding and inconsolable irritability in a young infant
trigger_question_examples: How is he feeding and settling compared to normal?
Is he taking his usual amount of milk?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's crying almost constantly and taking a lot less milk than he normally would. He just can't seem to get comfortable.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Parents applying thick occlusive natural oils (olive/coconut) to the scalp — driver of the flare
trigger_question_examples: Have you put anything on his skin or scalp — creams, oils, home remedies?
What have you been using to try to soften or treat the crusts?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We've been rubbing olive and coconut oil into his scalp quite thickly to try to loosen the crusts — everyone said that helps.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No thick yellow/green pus, no rapidly spreading erythema onto neck or chest, no hot swollen spreading edge — pertinent negatives against secondary bacterial infection/cellulitis
trigger_question_examples: Is there any thick yellow or green pus, or is the redness spreading quickly onto his neck or chest?
Does the red skin feel hot and swollen with a spreading edge?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's no thick pus, it's just clear weeping. The redness is only behind his ears and on his face, it hasn't spread to his neck or chest.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm not sure what to look for.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rash distribution involves scalp, forehead, eyebrows, nasolabial folds and post-auricular folds, with relative sparing of the cheeks and limbs
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the rash — is it mainly on his scalp and face folds, or on his cheeks, arms and legs?
Is the rash in the greasy areas like the eyebrows and sides of the nose?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on the top of his head, his forehead, his eyebrows, the creases beside his nose and behind his ears. His cheeks and his arms and legs are pretty much clear.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No petechiae/purpura, no failure to thrive — pertinent negatives against Langerhans cell histiocytosis
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any tiny purple or blood spots in the rash that don't fade when pressed?
Has he been growing and gaining weight normally until now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No purple or blood spots that I've seen, just the red and yellow crusty areas. He was growing and gaining weight fine until this started.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Please just tell us — has this turned into a serious infection? Do we need to take him to the emergency department tonight?
